data.title: Striking Boeing employees in Missouri turn down latest offer
data.description:
• Boeing (NYSE:BA) union workers who work on defense aircraft at several plants in Missouri have rejected the latest contract offer from the aerospace giant. 
  • The rebuff comes after management and IAM Union District 837 Negotiating Committee on Sept. 10 said they had come to a tentative, 5-year agreement that would have boosted the average wage to $109K from $75K, as well as a $4K ratification bonus.
  • A Boeing briefing sheet on the offer states that the new offer represents 45% average wage growth, a 5% increase from a prior offer made in late July.
  • The union told KMOV-TV that the latest offer's signing bonus wasn't as high as other workers have received, and lacked an increase in 401k benefits.
  • About 3,200 Boeing employees in Missouri began their strike on Aug. 4.

data.title: BlackRock exec Rick Rieder rises in contender rankings for Fed chair - report
data.description:
Rick Rieder, who oversees asset management giant BlackRock's (NYSE:BLK) fixed income business, is climbing in the the list of potential successors to Jerome Powell as Federal Reserve chair, according to a media report published Friday. 

U.S. Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent interviewed Rieder for the role during a two-hour meeting in New York, discussing monetary policy, the Fed's structure, and regulatory oversight, Bloomberg reported, citing an administration official.

Bessent praised Rieder's extensive markets background, as well as his leadership of large investment teams, his grasp of both micro- and macroeconomic forces, and his strength in applying forward-looking frmaeworks to gauge the economy, a person familiar with Bessent's thinking told Bloomberg.

So far, Bessent has sat down with four of the 11 publicly identified contenders for the Fed chair role, and the roster is expected to grow by another one or two names, the administration official said, the article said.

It was reported Thursday that Bessent this week has been meeting with a few candidates on President Donald Trump's shortlist for the next Fed chair, including former Fed governors Lawrence Lindsey and Kevin Warsh, as well as former St. Louis Fed President James Bullard.

Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

data.title: Catalyst Watch: Fed meeting, eyes on Meta Connect, FedEx earnings, and triple-witching day
data.description:
Welcome to Seeking Alpha's Catalyst Watch - a breakdown of some of next week's actionable events that stand out. Read more about the events that may impact stock prices next week.

 

Monday - September 15

  • Volatility watch - Asset Entities (ASST) and loanDepot (LDI) are set up for a volatile week of trading based on options volume. The most overbought stocks per their 14-day relative strength index include Zomedica (OTC:ZOMDF), Tourmaline Bio (TRML), and Sandisk (SNDK). The most oversold stocks per their 14-day relative strength index include Portillo’s (PTLO), Neonode (NEON), and Iridium Communications (IRDM).
  • IPO watch - StubHub (STUB) is expected to price its IPO and begin to trade. The online marketplace company is offering 34 million in an expected range of $22 to $25 per share. WaterBridge Infrastructure (WBI), Netskope (NTSK), and Regentis Biomaterials (RGNT) are also expected to start trading during the week. The post-IPO quiet periods end for Yimutian (YMT) and Velo3D (VELO) to free up analysts to post ratings.
  • Earnings watch - Notable companies due to report include Dave & Buster's (PLAY) and Hain Celestial (HAIN).
  • Dividend watch - Companies that have an ex-dividend date coming next week include Coca-Cola (KO), Domino's Pizza (DPZ), Gilead (GILD), and UnitedHealth Group (UNH).

 

Tuesday - September 16

  • All day - Workday (NASDAQ:WDAY) will host its financial analyst day as part of Workday Rising, the company's annual flagship conference for finance, HR, and IT professionals.
  • All day - Ralph Lauren Corporation (RL) will host an investor meeting. At the event, CEO Patrice Louvet and other members of the leadership team will provide an update on the company's strategic growth plan and key initiatives.
  • All day - Cimpress (CMPR) will host its 2025 annual Investor Day.
  • All day - The business of sports will be on display at the Front Office Sports Live event. Featured speakers include NBA Commissioner Adam Silver, MLB Commissioner Rob Manfred, ESPN (DIS) Chairman Jimmy Pitaro, and TNT Sports CEO Luis Silberwasser.
  • All day - Airbnb (ABNB) CEO Brian Chesky and Booking Holdings (BKNG) CEO Glenn Fogel will be speakers at the Skift Global Forum.
  • All day - The Las Vegas HR Tech Conference will take place in Las Vegas. Analysts tipped that the event could have some readthroughs for Workday (NASDAQ:WDAY), Oracle (ORCL), and Paycom Software (PAYC).
  • All day - Emerson (EMR), CSX (CSX), and Analog Devices (ADI) are some of the notable companies participating in the two-day J.P. Morgan 16th Annual U.S. All Stars Conference.
  • 8:30 a.m. The U.S. Census Bureau will release the July Retail Sales report.

 

Wednesday - September 17

  • Earnings watch - Notable companies due to report include General Mills (GIS) and Bullish (BLSH).
  • All day - Meta (NASDAQ:META) will hold its flagship Meta Connect conference for developers, highlighting broader innovations in AI, XR, and wearable technology. The biggest buzz could be over Meta's next generation of smart glasses. The event could impact a wide range of tech companies, including Microsoft (MSFT), Nvidia (NVDA), Apple (AAPL), Unity Software (U), and Qualcomm (QCOM).
  • All day - Notable investor events include Zoom Communications (ZM) holding its flagship Zoomtopia conference, MongoDB (MDB) holding an investor session, and monday.com's (MNDY) Investor Day event.
  • 9:05 a.m. Frontier Airlines (ULCC) CEO Brian Biffle will speak at the Skift Global Forum.
  • 2:00 p.m. The Federal Reserve will announce its interest rate decision.
  • 2:30 p.m. Federal Reserve Chairman Jerome Powell will hold a press conference.
  • 4:50 p.m. Royal Caribbean (RCL) CEO Jason Liberty will speak at the Skift Global Forum.
  • 5:00 p.m. Cracker Barrel Old Country Store (CBRL) will hold its earnings conference call. Management may add more details on its decision to halt its store and restaurant remodels.

 

Thursday - September 18

  • Earnings watch - Notable companies due to report include FedEx (NYSE:FDX), Lennar (LEN), Darden Restaurants (DRI), and FactSet Research (FDS).
  • All day -  Notable investor events include GitLab (GTLB) participating in AWS Cloud Day Dublin, Intuit (INTU) hosting its annual Investor Day at its headquarters in Mountain View, California, and DuPont (DD) hosting its Investor Day event.
  • All day - Continental AG (OTCPK:CTTAF) (OTCPK:CTTAY) is spinning off its automotive division. The new standalone company will be named Aumovio and will trade on the Frankfurt Stock Exchange.
  • All day - It is the one-year anniversary of the closing of the merger between Alaska Airlines (ALK) and Hawaiian Airlines.
  • All day - The CDC's Advisory Committee on Immunization Practices will hold a vaccine meeting.
  • 8:30 a.m. The Philadelphia Federal Index report will be released.

 

Friday - September 19

  • All day - The stock market could see extra volatility with triple witching day featuring the simultaneous expiration of stock options, stock index futures, and stock index options contracts.
  • All day - Faraday Future (FFAI) will hold its 919 Futurist Day and Stockholders' Community Day.
  • All day - Shareholders of WK Kellogg (NYSE:KLG) will vote on the takeover offer from Ferrero Group.

 

Seeking Alpha Editor Josh Fineman contributed to this story

data.title: Tech Voices: Tesla chair on Musk's future, Exxon's EV battery upgrade
data.description:
Seeking Alpha's roundup of statements, announcements and remarks that could impact the technology sector. 

  • Tesla (NASDAQ:TSLA) Chair Robyn Denholm said that CEO Elon Musk is the only one who can oversee the company's transition into AI and robotics, calling him a "generational leader."

"There aren’t any other people out there like Elon who can actually lead the company over the next decade or so," Denholm told Bloomberg TV on Friday. 

Denholm added that Musk could end up transitioning into the role of chief product officer and that such a move was contemplated in his recently disclosed compensation package.

She said Tesla had a contingency plan in place to appoint a new CEO if Musk were to suddenly leave the position. The company has also established a process to identify Musk's eventual successor.

“We want to make sure that we have the right leaders in place to do an orderly transition at some point in the future,” Denholm told Bloomberg. “And so making that part of this plan was a very deliberate activity.”

  • Denholm also said that Musk's political activities were his own business, despite concerns that they have contributed to slumping sales in certain markets.

“What he does from a personal perspective in terms of his political motivations is up to him,” Denholm said during her interview with Bloomberg. “From a politics perspective, obviously we’re in a democracy, so everybody gets to voice their points of view.”

  • Exxon Mobil (NYSE:XOM) CEO Darren Woods said his company has invented a new type of graphite that can significantly boost EV battery life.

“We’ve invented a new carbon molecule that will extend the life of the battery by 30%,” Woods told listeners at a university event on Friday, according to Bloomberg. "It’s a “revolutionary step change in battery performance.”

Woods added that the product was currently being tested by several EV makers.

data.title: Gemini stock briefly spikes 45% in Nasdaq debut
data.description:
Gemini Space Station (NASDAQ:GEMI), the crypto exchange run by Tyler and Cameron Winklevoss, saw its stock open as much as 45% above the initial offering price, before paring gains shortly thereafter.

Shares were up 21% at 2:43 p.m. ET. A day earlier, the company offered 15.2M shares at $28 apiece and raised $425M. The IPO pricing was had been upwardly revised from the previous marketed range of $24-$26.

Gemini's (NASDAQ:GEMI) listing on the Nasdaq joins a broader run of crypto firms tapping public markets, riding momentum from a more welcoming U.S. regulatory environment.

Shares of Figure Technology Solutions (FIGR), a blockchain-based lender, gapped up well above their IPO price in their first day of trading Thursday.

data.title: COVID vaccine names take a hit after report of child deaths CDC presentation
data.description:
Companies that manufacture authorized COVID-19 shots are down significantly following word that a closely watched CDC vaccines advisory committee meeting next week will receive a presentation tying the vaccines to the deaths of 25 children.

Moderna (NASDAQ:MRNA), Pfizer (NYSE:PFE)/BioNTech (NASDAQ:BNTX), and Novavax (NASDAQ:NVAX) are all in the red in Friday afternoon trading. Moderna and Pfizer/BioNTech's shots are based on an mRNA platform, while Novavax's (NASDAQ:NVAX) is a more traditional protein-based.

As of 230p ET, Moderna and BioNTech are down the most, with both off ~8%.

While The Washington Post first broke the news of the presentation, a subsequent report in The New York Times said that it will be made by Tracy Beth Hoeg, a medical doctor who is senior advisor for clinical sciences in the U.S. FDA's Office of the Commissioner and Center for Biologics Evaluation and Research.

During the COVID pandemic, she was a was a consultant epidemiologist to Florida Surgeon General Joseph Ladapo, who faced harsh criticism based on comments he made about COVID mitigation efforts, treatments, and misinformation. 

Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

data.title: Union Pacific works the White House as it looks for regulatory clearance on the Norfolk Southern deal
data.description:
Union Pacific Corp. (NYSE:UNP) CEO Jim Vena discussed the impact of the company's proposed $72 billion acquisition of Norfolk Southern Corp. (NYSE:NSC) with President Donald Trump in the White House on Friday. Reports indicate the meeting went well for Union Pacific (NYSE:UNP).

Earlier in the week, Vena indicated that senior administration officials understand the value of what the company is proposing, and they think it's an absolute win for the U.S. The deal is opposed by some transportation industry watchers due to the impact on competition and freight costs.

Union Pacific (UNP) is looking at acquiring Norfolk Southern in an $85 billion stock-and-cash transaction that would create the first coast-to-coast U.S. railroad by connecting over 50,000 miles of track across 43 states. The deal values Norfolk Southern (NYSE:NSC) at $320 per share and gives its shareholders cash plus 27% of the combined entity. The deal is seen as boosting supply chain efficiencies and regaining market share from trucking companies. The agreement received unanimous board approval but still requires Surface Transportation Board review. If approved, the deal is not expected to close until the early part of 2027.

Norfolk Southern (NSC) edged 0.6% higher in afternoon trading, while Union Pacific (UNP) was down 1.1%.

Market caps in the rails industry: Union Pacific (UNP) $127.2 billion, Canadian Pacific Kansas City Limited (CP) $69.2 billion, Norfolk Southern (NSC) $61.9 billion, CSX Corp (CSX) $60.8 billion, and Canadian National Railway (CNI) $58.5 billion. Berkshire Hathaway's (BRK.A) (BRK.B) BNSF Railway is estimated to have a value of around $80 billion.

data.title: Jamf seesaws on report it's exploring a sale
data.description:
Device management software firm Jamf (NASDAQ:JAMF) quickly rose and is now down 2% after a report that it's exploring a sale. 

Jamf, which is backed by private equity firm Vista Equity with a 34% stake, is working with bankers at Citi on the sale process, according to a  Reuters report on Friday, which cited people familiar with the matter. The company has been taking offers from potential buyers in recent months. Vista would exit its position as part of the sale. 

Jamf (NASDAQ:JAMF) has a market value of ~$1.4 billion. Its shares have dropped 23% this year.

Jamf didn't respond to Reuters request for comment.

data.title: U.S. stocks mixed as investors look for direction with Fed decision looming
data.description:
Wall Street's major averages were mixed on Friday, after the S&P 500 (SP500), the Nasdaq (COMP:IND), and the Dow (DJI) achieved new record closes a day before.

Now investors await the Federal Reserve's decision on interest rate cuts, with the markets pricing odds of a 25-basis-point rate cut next week at 92.5%, according to the CME FedWatch Tool. 

The benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) was last +0.1% in afternoon trade, while the Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) was +0.5% and hitting intraday highs, and the Dow (DJI) was -0.4%

“If the Fed doesn't cut rates multiple times, the economy, especially the job market, will get into even more trouble. If the Fed cuts rates multiple times, the risk of inflation rising again painfully is imminent. In short: the Fed is trapped,” said Marc-André Fongern, former Deutsche Bank and Goldman Sachs investment banker.

In addition, the U.S. Consumer Sentiment Index fell to 55.4 in September from 58.2 in August, missing the 54.9 consensus.

Over in the bond market, the 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) rose 4 basis points to 4.07%, while the 2-year yield (US2Y) rose 2 basis points to 3.57%.

Among the top gainers on Friday, Warner Bros. Discovery (WBD) and Paramount Skydance (PSKY) were +19.1% and +5.9% respectively on the announcement that the latter is preparing a majority cash bid for the entertainment giant.

U.S. stocks set another record closing on Thursday, with the Dow Jones (DJI) reaching the 46,000 level for the first time, after the consumer inflation report came higher on a monthly basis in August, while core CPI was steady.

More on markets:

data.title: Sunstone Hotel parts worth more than whole - analyst
data.description:
Sunstone Hotel (NYSE:SHO) should consider a sale as the sum of its parts is worth more than its whole, according to a Truist analyst, after an investor called on the company to consider selling itself. 

Earlier on Friday, Tarsadia, which owns a 3.4% stake in Sunstone Hotel (NYSE:SHO), called on the hotel REIT to to sell itself or liquidate its assets. Tarsadia also wants the real estate investment trust to appoint new directors to its 8-member board.

"As of yesterday's close, SHO shares were trading at 12.7x 2025 EV/EBITDA and 12.3x 2026E," Truist analyst Charles Scholes, who has a hold rating and $10 price target on Sunstone, wrote in a note on Friday. "However, we believe the underlying assets are worth at minimum 14x which would be worth ~$12/share; each 100 bps."

Likely buyers of the individual assets include private equity and/or high net worth individuals/families/sovereign wealth looking for trophy assets, according to Scholes. He  sees a smaller pool of company buyers as high interest rates create higher thresholds for go-private transactions. 

"This activism is not 100% a surprise given share performance for SHO and hotel REITS overall and a related disconnect between underlying asset valuation and equity valuation," Scholes added.
